target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## Summary

Preserves structured non-image tool-result content in provider replay/model-visible payloads instead of dropping it into empty or image-placeholder-style output. This addresses the provider-serializer slice of #96857 across OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses / transport replay, Anthropic Messages / transport replay, Google / Vertex, and Mistral conversion paths.

This PR intentionally does **not** claim to resolve the broader provider-failover, truncation, persistent session-degradation, or empty non-image result placeholder paths tracked separately in #96857 and related PRs.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where provider serializers only preserved explicit `type: "text"` tool-result blocks when building model-visible replay context. Structured non-image results such as MCP `resource` blocks, JSON status payloads, and runtime adapter output could be silently dropped before reaching the model, so the agent could receive an empty result or a placeholder instead of the actual structured output.

The affected paths include OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses / runtime transport replay, Anthropic Messages / runtime transport replay, Google / Vertex, and Mistral provider conversion.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix extracts a shared bounded structured tool-result serializer and uses it in provider replay plus the existing runtime visible-output structured fallback. That keeps the provider and runtime behavior aligned while ensuring structured payloads are safe before replay: sensitive fields are redacted, opaque/binary values are omitted, circular references are handled, and very large payloads are capped.

## User Impact

Agents can now read ordinary structured tool output in affected provider contexts instead of losing it during replay. Existing text blocks remain preferred, and image/audio payloads continue using the existing multimodal handling rather than being serialized as text.

## Implementation

- Adds `src/logging/tool-result-serialization.ts` as the shared bounded structured tool-result serializer.
- Uses the serializer from `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.ts` before provider replay.
- Reuses the same serializer from `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.ts` for runtime structured-result visible output.
- Preserves text blocks and skips image/audio blocks for text extraction.
- Redacts sensitive keys such as authorization, cookies, API keys, access/refresh/id tokens, client secrets, private keys, passwords, and generic token/secret fields.
- Omits opaque replay-only fields such as `encrypted_content` / `encrypted_stdout` and binary fields such as blobs or media `data` payloads.
- Handles circular structured payloads and caps serialized provider text.
- Keeps actual data URI tokens redacted without rewriting ordinary prose such as `metadata:ready` or `data: is ordinary prose`.
- Restores `CHANGELOG.md`; release-owned changelog content is not changed by this PR.

## Evidence

- **Code path proof — OpenAI Chat Completions provider payload:** `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` now captures the actual Chat Completions request payload via `onPayload` and verifies structured tool output is replayed as tool text after sanitization. The assertions prove:
  - JSON/resource tool-result blocks are serialized into the provider `messages[].content` field.
  - `authorization`, `token`, and `password` values are redacted to `***`.
  - binary `application/octet-stream` data is replaced with `[binary omitted: ... chars]`.
  - opaque `encrypted_content` is replaced with `[opaque data omitted: ... chars]`.
  - circular references serialize as `[Circular]`.
  - oversized structured output is capped with `…(truncated)…`.

- **Code path proof — OpenAI Responses provider conversion:** `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` now exercises `convertResponsesMessages()` and verifies the actual `function_call_output.output` payload contains sanitized structured text instead of raw secrets/binary/opaque data.

- **Shared sanitizer proof:** `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` covers structured-only blocks, mixed text/structured/image blocks, ordinary `data:` prose preservation, actual data URI token redaction, nested sensitive field redaction, opaque/binary omission, circular references, and length caps.

- **Runtime path alignment:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.ts` now delegates structured fallback serialization to the same shared serializer, preserving the runtime display path semantics while removing duplicated sanitizer logic.

- **Local lightweight validation on the pushed commit:**

```text
git diff --check
node --experimental-strip-types --check src/logging/tool-result-serialization.ts
node --experimental-strip-types --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.ts
node --experimental-strip-types --check src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.ts
node --experimental-strip-types --check src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts
node --experimental-strip-types --check src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts
```

All listed lightweight checks passed locally. Full lint/type/test coverage is left to GitHub Actions.

## Labels considered

- `agents` — affected behavior is model-visible agent/provider replay.
- `P1` — fixes loss of ordinary structured tool output in affected provider paths.
- `status: 📣 needs proof` — addressed here with provider-payload tests and PR body evidence.
- `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary` — relevant because provider replay now serializes structured payloads, so redaction/omission/caps are part of the patch.
- `merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery` — relevant because agent-visible tool output can affect message/tool workflows.

## Breaking changes

None.

## Risk

Low/medium. This changes model-visible serialization for structured non-image tool-result blocks. Risk is bounded by preserving explicit text-first behavior, continuing to skip image/audio payloads for text extraction, and applying shared redaction/omission/circular/cap handling before provider replay.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore provider serializers to the previous text-only extraction behavior. No migration, persistent state format change, dependency change, or configuration rollback is required.

## Docs updated

No user-facing docs were changed. `CHANGELOG.md` is not part of this PR diff; release notes can be handled by the release process or squash/merge message.

## Related issue / PR

- Related to #96857. This PR addresses only the provider serializer/replay slice.
- Related/overlapping PRs: #96895 covers a narrower provider serializer slice; #97423 covers empty non-image placeholder behavior. This PR does not replace those broader/adjacent scopes unless maintainers choose to consolidate.
- Review context addressed: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/97450#issuecomment-4825429953.

## Not tested / Limitations

- No live external provider request was made for this update.
- The broader #96857 provider-failover/session-corruption/truncation paths are not proven by this PR and should remain tracked separately unless maintainers decide this provider serializer slice is sufficient for part of the issue.

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a public plugin runtime task-run lifecycle API, exposes taskKind in task-run DTOs, changes task creation identity to include taskKind, and updates docs/tests for plugin-owned task records.

target.title: [codex] Add plugin task lifecycle API
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can inspect task runs today, but they do not have a narrow public SDK path for creating a plugin-owned task run, reporting progress, and finalizing the run while preserving owner/session scoping. That leaves plugin task lifecycle behavior either unavailable or tied to internal core task APIs that are not safe for third-party plugin contracts.

This PR adds a plugin-safe task run lifecycle API under `api.runtime.tasks.runs.bindSession(...).lifecycle` / `fromToolContext(...).lifecycle`. Plugin-created records are stored as existing `cli` task runtime rows with a required plugin-namespaced `taskKind`, and task creation identity now includes `taskKind` so same-owner/same-run records with different plugin task kinds stay separated.

## Summary

- Adds a plugin-safe task run lifecycle API under `api.runtime.tasks.runs.bindSession(...).lifecycle` / `fromToolContext(...).lifecycle`.
- Creates plugin-owned records as existing `cli` task runtime rows with required plugin-namespaced `taskKind`.
- Adds owner-scoped exact create/progress/finalize behavior and exposes `taskKind` in task run DTOs.
- Documents plugin-owned task records and SDK lifecycle usage.

## Evidence

Focused validation:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  71 passed (71)
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-tasks.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  29 passed (29)
```

```text
$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
```

```text
$ pnpm check:changed
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, docs
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Additional checks run:

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`

Redacted real runtime lifecycle proof at PR head `95cedbf4558905e68f351613f71fe7640df5b69e`, using `createRuntimeTaskRuns().bindSession(...).lifecycle` with an isolated temp state dir:

```text
$ pnpm tsx --eval '<runtime lifecycle create/progress/finalize proof script>'
{
  "created": {
    "runtime": "cli",
    "sessionKey": "agent:proof:telegram:group:redacted",
    "ownerKey": "agent:proof:telegram:group:redacted",
    "taskKind": "proof-plugin.session",
    "sourceId": "proof-plugin",
    "runId": "proof-run-redacted",
    "status": "running",
    "progressSummary": "created"
  },
  "progressed": {
    "runtime": "cli",
    "taskKind": "proof-plugin.session",
    "runId": "proof-run-redacted",
    "status": "running",
    "progressSummary": "progressed"
  },
  "finalized": {
    "runtime": "cli",
    "taskKind": "proof-plugin.session",
    "runId": "proof-run-redacted",
    "status": "succeeded",
    "progressSummary": "progressed",
    "terminalSummary": "finalized"
  },
  "sameKindReuse": {
    "sameTaskId": true,
    "taskKind": "proof-plugin.session",
    "runId": "proof-run-redacted"
  },
  "differentTaskKindSeparation": {
    "differentTaskId": true,
    "firstTaskKind": "proof-plugin.session",
    "secondTaskKind": "proof-plugin.other",
    "sharedRunId": "proof-run-redacted"
  }
}
```

This proves create, progress, finalize, same-kind reuse, and different-`taskKind` separation through the public plugin runtime lifecycle API.

## Known Base Failure

- `pnpm test:changed` routes `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` and fails `loadOpenClawPlugins > does not re-register non-bundled plugins after gateway-bindable boot loads` with expected `1`, received `2`.
- The same failure reproduces on a fresh `origin/main` worktree at `9e01d19db3`, so it is not introduced by this PR.

target.title: fix: making typing start fire-and-forget allows cleanup/idle to run before a persistent typin...
target.body:
## Summary

Found one concrete regression: making typing start fire-and-forget allows cleanup/idle to run before a persistent typing indicator has finished starting, so the stop path can no-op and leave the indicator behind.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: Pending typing start can outlive cleanup and leave persistent indicators stuck** (regression)
  - File: `src/channels/typing.ts:79`
  - Evidence: `onReplyStart` now starts `fireStart()` in the background and returns after one microtask at `src/channels/typing.ts:79-87`, while `fireStop` immediately marks the callbacks closed and calls `stop` at `src/channels/typing.ts:90-98`. For Feishu, `start` assigns `typingState` only after `await addTypingIndicator(...)` at `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts:176`; `stop` returns without doing anything if `typingState` is still unset at `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts:183-185`. A fast cleanup while `addTypingIndicator` is still in flight therefore calls stop too early, then the delayed start completes and records a persistent reaction with no later removal.
  - Impact: Feishu typing reactions are explicitly persistent until removed. A quick reply, `NO_REPLY`, empty-message cleanup, cancellation, or send-policy path can leave a visible typing reaction/indicator on the parent message after the run is already finished.
  - Suggested fix: keep typing start off the reply critical path, but serialize cleanup against the pending start. For example, track the current start promise and, if stop/cleanup happens while it is pending, run `stop` again after the promise settles when a start actually completed. Alternatively make persistent-indicator channels expose an abortable/pending-aware start helper.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/channels/typing.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/typing.ts`
- `src/channels/typing.test.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d
- Latest main at intake: 8093ae60292f4d9009b3d48f234b39b5cbc538e5
- Original commit author: Ayaan Zaidi
- GitHub author: @obviyus
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `40b0b1bfe051072f47d0160eb078afa3b17cef48..45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d`
- Changed files: `src/auto-reply/reply/typing.ts`, `src/channels/typing.ts`, `src/channels/typing.test.ts`
- Code read: changed files in full; `src/channels/typing-lifecycle.ts`; `src/channels/typing-start-guard.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`; relevant channel typing call sites, including Feishu, Matrix, Telegram, Discord, Signal, Mattermost, MSTeams, Zalo, and heartbeat typing.
- Dependencies/web: no dependency files changed; no web lookup needed.

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm install` was needed because `vitest/package.json` was missing.
- `pnpm test src/channels/typing.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/typing-persistence.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` passed.
- Focused `pnpm exec tsx -e ...` race probe reproduced the stale persistent-state ordering.

Known review limits:
- I did not run a live Feishu API test; the finding is based on the current callback ordering and Feishu’s local persistent-reaction state contract.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.

target.title: fix: macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression. The new guest script writer creates a root-owned `700` script, then the macOS lane executes it as the desktop user.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Low: macOS update script is not readable by the user that executes it** (reliability)
  - File: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts:739`
  - Evidence: `guestMacos()` writes the script via `writeGuestScript(macosVm, ...)`, then runs it through `prlctl exec ... --current-user ... /bin/bash <scriptPath>` or the sudo desktop-user fallback at lines 451-453. But `writeGuestScript()` writes with plain `prlctl exec <vm> /usr/bin/tee <scriptPath>` and then `chmod 700 <scriptPath>` at lines 730-739. The Parallels docs describe `--current-user` as logging in as the current guest OS user, so the write path and execute path are different identities for the macOS lane. ([docs.parallels.com](https://docs.parallels.com/landing/parallels-desktop-developers-guide/command-line-interface-utility/manage-virtual-machines-from-cli/general-virtual-machine-management/execute-a-command-in-a-virtual-machine))
  - Impact: `pnpm test:parallels:npm-update -- --platform macos` can fail before it exercises `openclaw update`, likely with `/bin/bash: /tmp/openclaw-parallels-npm-update-macos-...sh: Permission denied`. That breaks the intended release/update smoke coverage on current `main`; no later commit in the touched files fixes it.
  - Suggested fix: for macOS, write/chmod the temp script through the same resolved transport used to execute it, or explicitly `chown` it to the resolved desktop user and keep permissions non-world-readable because the script embeds provider credentials. Keep the Linux root-owned path separate.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066
- Latest main at intake: e311ffdcb94e760796a0a8a2c7e58fb8223678bf
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger

- Highest severity: low
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `231e5c618f8ae700d51d080e4182a57588da888b..af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066`
- Changed files: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`, `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- Code read: full changed files, `scripts/AGENTS.md`, Parallels smoke skill, adjacent guest transport helpers, macOS/Linux smoke helpers, package script entrypoint
- Dependencies/web: official Parallels `prlctl exec` docs; no package dependency changes
- Commands: `pnpm docs:list`, `git show`, `git diff`, `git diff --check`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm exec tsx scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts --help`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`

## Expected validation

ClawSweeper already ran:

target.title: fix: Mattermost interaction token forgeable via hardcoded HMAC...
target.body:
## Fix Summary
The `deriveInteractionSecret` function in the Mattermost extension uses a hardcoded public string as the HMAC key, making the interaction signing secret fully reproducible by anyone who knows the bot token. An attacker who obtains the bot token can forge valid `_token` values, craft arbitrary button-click POST requests to the gateway's interaction endpoint, and trigger agent system events (`core.system.enqueueSystemEvent`) as if a legitimate user clicked an approved Mattermost button.

## Issue Linkage
Fixes #64545

## Security Snapshot
- CVSS v3.1: 7.1 (High)
- CVSS v4.0: 7.1 (High)

## Implementation Details
### Files Changed
- `extensions/mattermost/src/config-schema-core.ts` (+1/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/config-schema.test.ts` (+19/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.test.ts` (+23/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.ts` (+8/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts` (+20/-17)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.ts` (+23/-10)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts` (+2/-3)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.ts` (+8/-2)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/types.ts` (+2/-0)

### Technical Analysis
1. Obtain the Mattermost bot token (available via the Mattermost admin panel, gateway environment variables, or any configuration leak).
2. Reproduce the derivation: `derivedSecret = HMAC-SHA256("openclaw-mattermost-interactions", botToken)` — this is deterministic and requires no further gateway access.
3. Construct an action context matching an existing interactive post, e.g., `{"action_id": "approve", "post_id": "<target_post_id>", "channel_id": "<target_channel_id>"}`.
4. Compute the forged token: `_token = HMAC-SHA256(derivedSecret, JSON.stringify(sortedContext))`.
5. POST a crafted payload to `http://<gateway-host>:<gatewayPort>/mattermost/interactions/<accountId>` with the forged `_token` field.
6. `verifyInteractionToken` at `interactions.ts:165-175` passes; the handler dispatches `core.system.enqueueSystemEvent` to the agent as if a legitimate user clicked an approved button.

## Validation Evidence
- Command: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
- Status: passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)

## Risk and Compatibility
- non-breaking; no known regression impact

## AI-Assisted Disclosure
- AI-assisted: yes
- Model: github-copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost interaction tokens should no longer be forgeable from knowledge of the bot token; the branch now uses a generated or configured interaction secret instead of the old deterministic `HMAC-SHA256("openclaw-mattermost-interactions", botToken)` derivation.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on the rebased PR head `e261bcfab6de7ff05d18d2493ea60aedf234498d`, running the real Mattermost interaction module through Node with `tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { createHmac } from "node:crypto"; import { ensureInteractionSecret, generateInteractionToken, verifyInteractionToken } from "./extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.ts"; const botToken = "redacted-bot-token-for-proof"; const oldDerivedSecret = createHmac("sha256", "openclaw-mattermost-interactions").update(botToken).digest("hex"); const generatedSecret = ensureInteractionSecret("proof-account"); const configuredSecret = ensureInteractionSecret("configured-account", "configured-proof-secret"); const context = { action_id: "approve", proof: "after-fix" }; const validToken = generateInteractionToken(context, "proof-account"); const oldForgedToken = createHmac("sha256", oldDerivedSecret).update(JSON.stringify(context)).digest("hex"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ generatedSecretLength: generatedSecret.length, generatedSecretIsHex: /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/.test(generatedSecret), generatedSecretMatchesOldBotTokenDerivation: generatedSecret === oldDerivedSecret, configuredSecretPreserved: configuredSecret === "configured-proof-secret", generatedTokenVerifies: verifyInteractionToken(context, validToken, "proof-account"), oldBotTokenDerivedForgeryVerifies: verifyInteractionToken(context, oldForgedToken, "proof-account") }, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "generatedSecretLength": 64,
  "generatedSecretIsHex": true,
  "generatedSecretMatchesOldBotTokenDerivation": false,
  "configuredSecretPreserved": true,
  "generatedTokenVerifies": true,
  "oldBotTokenDerivedForgeryVerifies": false
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The generated interaction secret is random-looking 64-character hex and does not equal the old bot-token-derived secret. A token generated with the new per-account secret verifies, while a token forged from the old bot-token-derived secret does not verify.
- What was not tested: Full live Mattermost server button-click delivery was not exercised in this local proof; focused local runtime proof and Mattermost extension tests were run for the changed interaction-secret behavior.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `228ade8544e`: rebased the full stack onto current `main`.** Conflict resolved (`scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` surface budgets reconciled to main's current values + this stack's deltas; `config-types` deprecated budget 416→417 for the new public `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type). The plugin-compat [P1] fix (source-aware host-FS default-deny) survives the rebase and is **re-proven against current main**: in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container, reverting only the resolver fix makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`, and restoring it passes — so the regression exists on current main and the fix removes it. Full gate green on the rebased head: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, oxlint clean, typecheck clean. Now `MERGEABLE` against main. The host-FS write boundary still needs explicit maintainer/security acceptance (a product decision, not an author fix).

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `acb9ab0a39d`: fix ClawSweeper [P1] plugin-name compatibility.** Adding `read`/`write`/`edit` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` name-denied any *same-named plugin tool* over `/tools/invoke` + SDK `tools.invoke`, so a pre-existing non-optional plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` returned 404 even with the host-FS opt-ins off. Per the resolver, the default-deny is now **source-aware** (`src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` `HOST_FS_BUILTIN_CODING_DENY_NAMES` + the gateway deny filter in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): a same-named tool whose *only* deny reason is the host-FS built-in coding default is preserved when it carries plugin metadata (`getPluginToolMeta`); the built-in coding tool (no plugin meta) stays gated behind the owner gate + opt-in, and owner-only / explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / `exec`/`fs_write`/etc. are unaffected. Two regression tests assert a plugin `read`/`write` stays reachable **without** a new `gateway.tools.allow` entry. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23; oxlint clean. Red/green proof: reverting only the resolver fix makes both new tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`. **Still pending: rebase onto current `main`** (main refactored the gateway deny-handling path, so the rebase needs careful re-expression — tracked separately).

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

Supplemental (not a substitute for the live proof above), all in the same `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container at head `afe36ccf456f`: unit tests — **160 passed** across the gateway test projects (`tools-invoke-http` 42 + `tool-resolution` 5 + `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19, ×3 projects = 7 file-projects); `lint` (oxlint, 0/0), `tsgo:core` (0), `check:test-types` (0), and `plugin-sdk-surface-report` vitest (4/4). **`checks-node-core-fast` / plugin-SDK surface budget:** this PR adds `hostFsWrite?` as a *field* on the existing `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type — **zero new top-level exports** — so `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` passes on the `+1` budget already carried by #85664's base commit (`config-types` 416 / public 10285 / public-fn 5164 / deprecated 3238). No further budget change in this PR.

**Not tested:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` (RCE-class; out of scope for this PR and never exposed by the direct-invoke factory).

target.title: fix: CLI auth status/probe path regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one regression in the CLI auth status/probe path. The commit intentionally makes unscoped external CLI discovery skip missing runtime-only profiles, but `models status` and `models status --probe` still load the auth store without passing the providers they are about to report or probe.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: `models status --probe` misses CLI-only credentials after scoped discovery** (regression)
  - File: `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:259`
  - Evidence: `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts:210` now returns false for unscoped missing external CLI profiles unless a matching stored OAuth profile already exists. `buildProbeTargets` still calls `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir)` without `allowKeychainPrompt: false`, `externalCliProviderIds`, or `externalCliProfileIds`, even though the function receives the exact `providers` and probe profile filters at `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:254-256`. A focused smoke with a temporary Codex CLI auth file confirmed `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore)` returns `[]`, while `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore, { providerIds: ["openai-codex"] })` returns `["openai-codex:default"]`.
  - Impact: A user who only has file-backed Codex/Claude/Minimax CLI credentials and no persisted OpenClaw auth profile can resolve runtime auth through the newly scoped model-auth path, but `openclaw models status --probe` will build no profile target for that same credential. This produces false “nothing to probe” or missing-auth diagnostics for the provider most likely being checked.
  - Suggested fix: In `buildProbeTargets`, load the store with a scope derived from the requested probe surface, for example `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir, { allowKeychainPrompt: false, config: cfg, externalCliProviderIds: providers, externalCliProfileIds: options.profileIds })`. The plain status path at `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts:223` should likewise pass the config-derived external CLI scope, matching `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts:296-301`.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Latest main at intake: 581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger
- GitHub author: @steipete
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `54e6e3d7daf5d0d857edf756b35628a29d11c7f5..581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12`
- Changed files: `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.ts`
- Code read: changed files, auth store overlay path, external auth overlay, auth profile order, CLI model status/probe callers, gateway auth-status caller
- Dependencies/web: no dependency changes; no web lookup needed

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- Focused external CLI resolver smoke with temporary Codex CLI auth file
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.targets.test.ts`
- `git diff --check 54e6e3d7daf5d0d857edf756b35628a29d11c7f5..581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12`

Known review limits:
- I did not run a full `models status --probe` live provider call, because the actionable regression is in target construction before network probing.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.

target.title: fix(agents): distinguish terminal aborts from retryable failures (#60388)
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `d189c5e08da`: rebased onto current `main` + reconciled the abort contract.** Resolved conflicts in `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts`. (1) **Restart-abort terminality preserved** — re-added `isAgentRunRestartAbortReason` to both `isTerminalAbort` paths so gateway restart/shutdown aborts stay terminal (addresses the ClawSweeper [P1] restart-abort regression); main's catch-site rethrow auto-survived. (2) **Reconciled with main's `isCallerAbortSignal`** — main now skips fallback on any caller-signal abort, so the two #60388 tests that asserted a *non-terminal caller abort still falls back* were updated to the current contract (caller abort → no fallback); the PR's distinct value — terminal detection on the runner's *internal* run-budget controller via `isTerminalAbortFromError` — is unchanged and still covered. (3) Removed now-orphaned `isFallbackAbortError`/`shouldRethrowAbort` helpers (dead after main's failover refactor). (4) `run.ts` conflict resolved by keeping main's `adoptActiveSessionId()` helper + this PR's `timedOutByRunBudget` flag. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container: the focused abort suite (`model-fallback`, `chat-abort`, `compact.abort-signal`, `failover-policy`, `assistant-failover`, `trajectory/metadata`) all green across 3 shards; oxlint clean; `git diff --check` clean.

---

Addresses #60388 (complementary to #52365, see "Relationship to PR #52365" below)

Today the fallback layer cannot tell the difference between two very different aborts:

1. **"This model failed, try another"** -> fallback should retry
2. **"The whole run is over"** -> fallback should stop immediately

Two situations where the run is over and retrying with another model wastes resources:

- **Run-budget timeout (#60388)**: The embedded runner's `scheduleAbortTimer` fires `runAbortController.abort(makeTimeoutAbortReason())` after the configured `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`. The budget is exhausted -- giving the next candidate ~0 ms remaining is guaranteed to fail and wastes API calls. The user issue description (#60388) says "On a fleet of ~100 cron jobs: 30+ model-fallback events per day, almost all triggered by run timeouts."

- **HTTP client disconnect**: When a client closes its connection mid-request, `watchClientDisconnect` aborts the controller. No caller is left to receive a response, so any tokens spent on a fallback model are wasted.

Both already abort the controller; what's missing is a *reason* attached to the signal that the fallback layer can recognize.

## Summary

- **Problem:** Run-budget timeout aborts (and HTTP client disconnects) currently flow through the model fallback chain as if they were retryable provider failures, wasting API calls and lane occupancy.
- **Why it matters:** Each unwanted fallback attempt holds a session lane and burns tokens for no benefit. On busy systems (#60388 reports 30+ events/day from cron timeouts alone) this is meaningful overhead.
- **What changed:** The fallback layer now checks `AbortSignal.reason` for two terminal markers (`name === "TimeoutError"` for run-budget, `name === "ClientDisconnectError"` for client disconnect) and short-circuits the chain when found.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to how aborts are *triggered*, no changes to per-provider timeout/retry semantics, no changes to AbortController lifecycles. Issues #37505 (cron AbortController sharing) and #58578 (mid-flight primary restore) are different abort *sources* upstream of the fallback layer and remain unchanged.

- [x] Bug fix

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue

- Closes #60388
- Refs #83962 (same cascade shape: "Control UI webchat request timeout aborts all fallback candidates" — error message `All models failed (4): ... aborted ... aborted ...` is exactly the pre-fix behavior this PR replaces with a single `decision=surface_error`)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `shouldRethrowAbort()` in `model-fallback.ts` checks `isFallbackAbortError(err) && !isTimeoutError(err)` -- which means timeout errors are *intentionally not* rethrown, so the fallback chain runs them. This is correct for per-provider timeouts (provider is slow, try another), but wrong for run-budget timeouts (whole run is out of time, no point retrying). The two cannot be told apart from the error alone.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No check for *why* the abort happened. The `signal.reason` carrying `TimeoutError` (set by `pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1382-1386` via `makeTimeoutAbortReason`) was never inspected.
- Contributing context: The HTTP client disconnect path added in #54388 has the same shape -- it tags the abort with no reason today, so a downstream client disconnect also flows through fallback retries.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: Six new tests under `describe("terminal abort propagation (closes #60388)")`:
  - `signal.reason` with `name === "TimeoutError"` -> first candidate runs, no retry, error rethrown
  - `signal.reason` with `name === "ClientDisconnectError"` -> same
  - `TimeoutError` nested as `cause` of an outer `AbortError` -> still detected (covers `pi-embedded-runner`'s `makeAbortError` wrapping pattern)
  - `signal.reason` with a generic error -> fallback runs normally (non-terminal)
  - No `abortSignal` passed -> fallback runs normally (back-compat for existing callers)
  - `abortSignal` provided but not aborted -> fallback runs normally (live-signal back-compat)
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The tests construct the abort signal directly and assert on `run.mock.calls.length === 1` to verify the chain stopped. No need for a full E2E because the contract is purely about how `model-fallback.ts` interprets `AbortSignal.reason`.
- Existing test that already covers this: None.
- If no new test is added, why not: 6 new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a run-budget timeout fires (the agent run exceeds `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`), the model fallback chain now stops immediately instead of trying further candidates. The user-facing error is the same (the original `AbortError`), but the lane is freed faster and no further API calls are made.

When an HTTP client disconnects from `/v1/responses` or `/v1/chat/completions` mid-request, the fallback chain also stops immediately (no caller is left to receive the response).

Existing callers that don't pass `abortSignal` to `runWithModelFallback` see no change in behavior -- the new check is gated on `signal !== undefined && signal.aborted`.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[run-budget timer fires]
  -> runAbortController.abort(TimeoutError)
  -> agent attempt throws AbortError
  -> shouldRethrowAbort(err) returns false (because isTimeoutError(err) is true)
  -> runFallbackCandidate returns { ok: false } -> tries next candidate
  -> next candidate also times out (~0ms budget left) -> tries next ...
  -> wasted API calls

After:
[run-budget timer fires]
  -> runAbortController.abort(TimeoutError)  // unchanged
  -> agent attempt throws AbortError
  -> isTerminalAbort(signal) returns true (signal.reason.name === "TimeoutError")
  -> shouldRethrowAbort(err, signal) returns true
  -> error rethrown immediately, no further candidates tried
```

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Docker linux/amd64)
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / OpenClaw built from this branch
- Test runner: Vitest

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with a small `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` (e.g. 5s) and one or more model fallbacks
2. Send a request that takes longer than the timeout
3. Observe the fallback chain behavior

### Expected (after fix)

- Primary candidate runs, hits the timeout, fallback chain stops, single error returned
- Logs show no `[model-fallback] candidate_failed` entries for fallback candidates beyond the first

### Actual (before fix)

- Primary candidate runs, hits the timeout
- Fallback layer tries the next candidate with ~0ms budget remaining
- Next candidate also times out, fallback layer tries the next, and so on
- 2-3x the API calls before the chain exhausts and returns an error

The contrast between A and B is the runtime delta the compact.ts:436 fix produces: when a terminal abort fires during compaction, the model-fallback chain stops at one call instead of emitting four `decision=candidate_failed` log lines against compaction-fallback models the caller no longer cares about.

**Latest-head cron pre-execution stall proof (against rebuilt `f5182213bb`):**

```text
[bundle] using model-fallback-GKGkpeHu.js
[bundle] contains NEW prefix 'cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start' : true
[bundle] contains prior 'cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start'     : true
[bundle] contains prior 'cron: job execution timed out'                                : true

=== A1. new prefix BARE — 'cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start' ===
  run() calls   = 1 (expected 1)
  outcome       = AbortError: aborted
  PASS

=== A2. new prefix PHASE-SUFFIXED — '... (last phase: runner_ready)' ===
  run() calls   = 1 (expected 1)
  outcome       = AbortError: aborted
  PASS

=== A3. existing prefix BARE — 'cron: job execution timed out' (sanity) ===
  run() calls   = 1 (expected 1)
  outcome       = AbortError: aborted
  PASS

=== A4. existing prefix PHASE-SUFFIXED — 'cron: isolated agent setup ... (last phase: workspace_provision)' (sanity) ===
  run() calls   = 1 (expected 1)
  outcome       = AbortError: aborted
  PASS

[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-provider/primary-model candidate=primary-provider/primary-model reason=rate_limit next=ollama/fb1-model detail=provider rate limited
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-provider/primary-model candidate=ollama/fb1-model reason=rate_limit next=ollama/fb2-model detail=provider rate limited
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-provider/primary-model candidate=ollama/fb2-model reason=rate_limit next=ollama/fb3-model detail=provider rate limited
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-provider/primary-model candidate=ollama/fb3-model reason=rate_limit next=none detail=provider rate limited

=== B1. NON-terminal provider failure (RateLimitError) — must cascade 4 times ===
  run() calls   = 4 (expected 4)
  outcome       = FallbackSummaryError: All models failed (4): primary-provider/primary-model: ...
  PASS

Summary:
  PASS  A1. new prefix BARE → 1 call (short-circuit, no decision=candidate_failed)
  PASS  A2. new prefix PHASE-SUFFIXED → 1 call
  PASS  A3. existing 'cron: job execution timed out' → 1 call (regression sanity)
  PASS  A4. existing 'cron: isolated agent setup ...' → 1 call (regression sanity)
  PASS  B1. non-terminal RateLimitError → 4 calls cascade (provider-retry semantics preserved)
```

The new prefix added in commit `f5182213bb` covers `preExecutionTimeoutErrorMessage()` (`src/cron/service/timer.ts:381/383`) — the isolated-agent pre-execution watchdog fires after setup completes but before the runner starts consuming the run budget. Both bare and phase-suffixed shapes short-circuit; non-terminal provider errors still cascade per the existing model-fallback contract.

**Latest-head Docker proof for the abortable() wrapper marker (head `8f484bcde5`):**

Setup: fresh container `openclaw-62682-proof-8f484bcd` from base image `openclaw:main` (linux/amd64), source mounted at `/workspace` at PR head `8f484bcde5`, `dist/` built via `pnpm build:docker`. Gateway banner inside the container confirms the head SHA:

```text
$ docker exec openclaw-62682-proof-8f484bcd node /workspace/openclaw.mjs --version
```
